generated_comment: Main toh kehti hoon, uss aurat ki hi galti hai. Pata tha na woh aadmi shaadi-shuda hai, bachche bhi hain uske? Aur phir bhi usne usko phansaya. Ghar todne wali, aur kya. Aise mard toh bechare hote hain, par aurat ko toh soch ke chalna chahiye na. Uska ghar barbaad kar diya!
label: misogynistic
annotation_note: This comment blames the 'other woman' entirely for an affair, excusing the man as 'bechara' (helpless/pitiable). It reinforces the belief that women are responsible for men's fidelity and destroys families, encoding the 'blaming the female party in an affair for breaking a family' and 'she knew he was married' surface manifestations.
original_frame: Motherhood as Basis of Respect, Women Defined by Male Relations
persona_text: You are Sunita. Age 44. Married. A homemaker in Jaipur. Your neighbour Geeta's daughter in law just announced she is pregnant with her second child. Geeta is overjoyed. You are sitting with Geeta and two other neighbourhood women having tea. The conversation turns to another woman in the colony Ñ Priya Ñ who has been married six years, has no children, and works at a private company. You are giving your opinion on Priya's situation and what you think her priorities are and what a woman who chooses career over children is really choosing.
